features.description:
Scaffold the TypeScript project, set up the MCP server with Streamable HTTP transport on port 3333, implement the MCP tool aggregator/proxy that connects to downstream MCPs via stdio child processes, and build the core daemon infrastructure (start/stop/status CLI commands, PID file management, SQLite schema initialization). By end of this feature the SERVICE daemon starts, exposes a valid MCP server at http://localhost:3333/mcp, and an AI agent can connect and call tools from a downstream test MCP.

KEY TASKS:
- Initialize package.json: name='service-mcp', type='module'. Deps: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, better-sqlite3, express, commander, chalk, ora, tsx. DevDeps: typescript, @types/node, @types/express, @types/better-sqlite3, jest, ts-jest, @types/jest
- tsconfig.json: target ES2022, module NodeNext, strict true, outDir dist/, rootDir src/
- src/db/schema.ts — SQL CREATE TABLE for: integrations, notifications, mcp_connections, agent_sessions, agent_notes + FTS5 virtual table
- src/db/database.ts — Database class wrapping better-sqlite3, opens ~/.service/service.db, runs migrations
- src/server/proxy.ts — StdioMcpProxy: spawns child process, JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, listTools(), callTool(), crash recovery with exponential backoff
- src/server/mcp-hub.ts — McpHub: McpServer + StreamableHTTPServerTransport on express POST /mcp (port 3333), aggregates downstream tools namespaced as '{mcpId}__{toolName}', routes tool calls
- src/daemon/manager.ts — start/stop/status + PID file at ~/.service/service.pid
- src/cli/index.ts — Commander: 'service start', 'service stop', 'service status', '--help'
- src/main.ts — daemon entry: init DB, McpHub, express servers (3333 + 3334 placeholder), SIGTERM handler
- bin/service.js — shebang entry
- Tests: proxy.test.ts, mcp-hub.test.ts, database.test.ts
- npm scripts: build (tsc), test (jest --forceExit), start (node dist/main.js), service (tsx src/cli/index.ts)

TECHNICAL NOTES:
- Use McpServer from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js + StreamableHTTPServerTransport
- ESM: all imports use .js extensions
- Tool namespacing: strip prefix when routing back to downstream proxy
- PID + state at ~/.service/
- Tests use --forceExit, mock child processes
expectedBehavior: npm run build exits 0, no TypeScript errors, dist/ created
npm test exits 0, all unit tests pass
node dist/main.js starts daemon, writes PID to ~/.service/service.pid
curl -X POST http://localhost:3333/mcp with initialize request returns valid MCP InitializeResult
POST /mcp with tools/list returns array including service__ built-in tools
node dist/cli/index.js stop cleanly stops the daemon
verificationSteps: node dist/main.js &; sleep 2; curl -X POST http://localhost:3333/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}'

features.description:
Build the 24/7 notification gateway: IntegrationAdapter interface, notification storage in SQLite, SSE push system for real-time agent delivery, and two built-in integrations (echo test + generic webhook). Extend the MCP hub with notification tools. After this feature a webhook POST creates a notification visible via SSE, MCP tool, and REST API — and notifications survive daemon restarts.

KEY TASKS:
- src/integrations/types.ts — IntegrationAdapter interface, NotificationEvent type, IntegrationConfig type with JSONSchema
- src/gateway/notification-store.ts — SQLite CRUD: insert, getRecent(limit, source?, unread?), markRead, getUnreadCount, search (FTS5). Config encryption: AES-256-GCM via Node.js crypto, key from ~/.service/.encryption_key
- src/gateway/event-bus.ts — EventEmitter singleton
- src/gateway/push-manager.ts — SSE connection pool, heartbeat every 30s, broadcasts to all on new event
- src/api/admin.ts — Express router port 3334: GET /health, GET /events (SSE), GET /api/notifications (paginated, ?unread, ?source), PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read, POST /webhooks/:integrationId
- src/integrations/builtin/echo.ts — synthetic events every 30s (configurable interval_seconds)
- src/integrations/builtin/webhook.ts — listens to POST /webhooks/:id, creates NotificationEvent
- src/integrations/registry.ts — load/unload adapters, stores configs in DB
- Extend McpHub: add service__get_notifications, service__get_unread_count, service__mark_notification_read
- Update main.ts: start both express servers (3333 MCP + 3334 admin), init all gateway components
- Tests: notification-store, push-manager, echo integration, webhook integration

SSE FORMAT: id: {uuid}\nevent: notification\ndata: {JSON}\n\n
Encryption: AES-256-GCM, random IV prepended to ciphertext, stored as BLOB
expectedBehavior: GET http://localhost:3334/health returns {status:'ok'}
POST http://localhost:3334/webhooks/test creates a notification
GET http://localhost:3334/api/notifications returns the notification
GET http://localhost:3334/events SSE stream stays open and delivers events
service__get_notifications MCP tool returns recent notifications
Notifications persist after daemon restart
verificationSteps: Restart daemon, curl /api/notifications — same notification still present

features.description: Address all 5 blocking issues found by the scrutiny-validator-m1 code review. COMPLETED via direct task session 5ec5c958. All fixes implemented in commit 8e94671: (1) CLI status now shows uptime/active integrations/connected MCPs via state.json + /api/status; (2) MCP Accept header relaxed via Proxy wrapper; (3) Daemon auto-loads MCP connections from DB on startup, /api/mcp-connections POST/DELETE added; (4) Webhook route routes through WebhookIntegration.receiveWebhook(); (5) IntegrationRegistry uses connecting→active→error state transitions.
expectedBehavior: service status shows uptime, active integrations count, connected MCPs count
MCP initialize returns 200 without explicit Accept header
Daemon auto-connects MCP connections saved in DB on startup
POST /api/mcp-connections saves and connects a downstream MCP
POST /webhooks/:id goes through the WebhookIntegration adapter
IntegrationRegistry only marks status=active after connect() succeeds
verificationSteps: npm run build && npm test — passes

features.description: Fix duplicate webhook notification persistence: admin.ts POST /webhooks/:integrationId was calling adapter.receiveWebhook() THEN also directly calling store.insert() + eventBus.emitNotification(), causing double notifications. Fixed by removing the duplicate calls in the registered-adapter path — only adapter.receiveWebhook() is called there. Implemented via direct task session f291deb7, commit 7cb7ef5. Build/test/lint all green (72 tests pass).
expectedBehavior: POST /webhooks/:integrationId creates exactly one notification (not two)
npm run build passes
npm test passes 72 tests

features.description: Fix A-MCP-03/04: downstream MCP tools added via addProxyById() not appearing in active MCP sessions. Root cause: McpHub stored transport references only (not McpServer instances), so dynamic tool additions weren't propagated to existing sessions. Fix: track SessionEntry with both server+transport per session; on addProxyById() call registerProxyToolsOnServer() on all active sessions; on removeProxy() call handle.remove() on all sessions. Also fixed inputSchema cast (z.object({}).passthrough()). Added 2 new tests. Commit c272554. Build/test(74)/lint all green.
expectedBehavior: POST /api/mcp-connections returns toolsAdded and tools appear in subsequent tools/list
Namespaced downstream tool calls work end-to-end
npm test passes 74 tests

features.description:
Build the integration management system with full CLI wizard, TUI dashboard, REST API for integration CRUD, and first-party adapter implementations for X/Twitter, Slack, Email (SMTP+IMAP), and HTTP-poll.

KEY TASKS:
- src/integrations/builtin/x-twitter.ts — twitter-api-v2: stream tweets, tools: tweet, reply, retweet, like, search_tweets
- src/integrations/builtin/slack.ts — @slack/bolt Socket Mode: message events, tools: send_message, list_channels, get_thread, react
- src/integrations/builtin/email.ts — nodemailer + imap-simple: poll inbox, tools: send_email, list_emails, read_email, reply_email
- src/integrations/builtin/http-poll.ts — fetch URL on schedule, SHA-256 hash change detection, tool: fetch_now
- Update IntegrationRegistry: add all 4 new types, add enable/disable methods
- src/cli/wizard.ts — inquirer-based wizard: schema-driven prompts from configSchema JSON Schema
- src/cli/dashboard.ts — blessed TUI: 4 panes (Live Feed, Integrations, MCPs, Agents), refreshes every 2s from admin API
- Extend CLI: integration list/add/remove/enable/disable, mcp list/add/remove, dashboard command
- Extend admin.ts: GET/POST/DELETE /api/integrations, GET /api/integrations/types, GET/POST/DELETE /api/mcp-connections, GET /api/status
- ajv validation of integration configs against configSchema
- Tests: wizard, each new adapter (mocked), dashboard smoke test
expectedBehavior: service integration list outputs table of integrations
service integration add webhook prompts for name and registers it
service integration remove <id> removes integration
service dashboard opens TUI without crash
GET /api/integrations/types returns all 6 integration types with configSchema
GET /api/mcp-connections returns downstream MCPs
Integration disable/enable works without daemon restart
verificationSteps: node dist/cli/index.js integration add webhook (non-interactive: pass --name test-webhook)

features.description:
Complete the full agent-facing MCP tool suite and reach ≥75% test coverage.

KEY TASKS:
- src/tools/memory-tools.ts — service__save_note, service__get_note, service__list_notes, service__delete_note (SQLite agent_notes table)
- src/tools/mcp-management-tools.ts — service__connect_mcp, service__disconnect_mcp, service__list_connected_mcps
- src/tools/search-tools.ts — service__search_notifications (FTS5)
- src/tools/platform-tools.ts — PlatformToolsManager: auto-generates MCP tools from active integration adapters, registers/deregisters dynamically on integration load/unload
- src/tools/status-tools.ts — service__service_status (full health info)
- src/tools/help-tools.ts — service__help (dynamic markdown guide of all tools)
- Update McpHub: addTool(name, desc, schema, handler) and removeTool(name) for dynamic registration
- Update main.ts: init PlatformToolsManager, register all tool modules
- Add tests for all new tools, run coverage and verify ≥75%
expectedBehavior: service__save_note and service__get_note round-trip works
service__list_connected_mcps returns current downstream MCPs
service__connect_mcp adds a new MCP and its tools appear in tools/list
service__search_notifications returns results matching query
service__service_status returns version, uptime, integration count
service__help returns formatted tool guide
npm test --coverage shows ≥75% coverage
verificationSteps: POST /mcp tools/list — verify all service__ tools present

features.description: Fix 6 blocking/non-blocking issues from m2 scrutiny: (1) X/Twitter real API wiring via twitter-api-v2 dynamic import; (2) Slack real API via @slack/bolt Socket Mode + WebClient; (3) Email real SMTP/IMAP via nodemailer + imap-simple with polling; (4) PlatformToolsManager wired to IntegrationRegistry lifecycle events (EventEmitter) in main.ts; (5) McpHub.removeTool iterates active sessions and calls handle.remove() for clean deregistration; (6) Dashboard 'Status' pane renamed 'Agents'. Commit 0c3e885. 255 tests pass, build/lint green.
expectedBehavior: X/Twitter adapter real API wiring (dynamic import)
Slack adapter uses @slack/bolt Socket Mode
Email adapter uses nodemailer + imap-simple polling
Integration load/unload events wire to PlatformToolsManager in runtime
removeTool cleans up handles in all active sessions
Dashboard shows Agents pane not Status

features.description: Fix 3 remaining m2 blocking issues: (1) PlatformToolsManager session registration: added registerSession/unregisterSession, McpHub.setPlatformToolsManager(), wired in main.ts; (2) McpHub tool handle tracking unified — session-init tools now stored in dynamicToolHandles for complete removeTool cleanup; (3) Added twitter-api-v2, @slack/bolt, nodemailer, imap-simple to package.json, removed mock-success fallbacks, adapters now throw/return explicit errors if packages unavailable. Commit 62f1583. 258 tests pass, build/lint green.
expectedBehavior: Platform tools from integrations propagate to active MCP sessions
removeTool cleans up all handle types in active sessions
Adapter packages in package.json, no mock-success responses
npm test passes 258 tests

features.description:
Final polish: comprehensive README, startup banner, shell completion, npm packaging, actionable error messages, example configs, GitHub Actions CI.

KEY TASKS:
- README.md: title, ASCII diagram, quickstart (5 steps), CLI reference, MCP tool reference, integration setup guides, claude-desktop-config.json example
- examples/: mcp-connections.json, webhook-integration.sh, claude-desktop-config.json, cursor-mcp-config.json
- docs/integrations/: per-integration setup guides
- Startup banner in main.ts: ASCII 'SERVICE' art, version, ports, integration count
- 'service completion [shell]' command for bash/zsh/fish
- 'service update' command: check npm registry for new version
- Error audit: all catch blocks → friendly messages via ServiceError + formatError
- package.json: bin, files, engines fields for npm publish
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: lint + build + test on push/PR
- LICENSE (MIT), CHANGELOG.md v1.0.0
- Final: npm run build ✓, npm test ✓, npm pack ✓
expectedBehavior: npm run build passes
npm test passes 100%
npm pack produces valid tarball
service --help shows all commands
README explains the system clearly
examples/claude-desktop-config.json shows correct MCP connection config
verificationSteps: npm run build && npm test && npm pack
